story.iWant: to update the message model to support parent-child relationships
story.soThat: messages can form a tree structure for branching conversations
story.tasks: - Update Message interface with tree fields (AC: #1, #5) - Update MessageRepository for tree queries (AC: #4) - Implement assembleContext() for branch path (AC: #2, #6) - Implement getNextBranchIndex() with limit check (AC: #3) - Update cache support for branched messages (AC: #1)

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Messages form valid tree via parentMessageId (TM §2.1)
Context assembly follows correct branch path by walking up tree (TM §2.3)
Branch limit (max 9 variations = branchIndex 0-9) enforced locally before API call (TM §2.2)
MessageRepository queries support tree operations (get by parent, get branches)
assembleContext() returns ordered array from root to current message

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.section: Data Models - Message Entity (Enhanced for Tree Structure)
doc.snippet: Tree structure fields: parentMessageId: string | null (null for root messages), branchIndex: number (0=original, 1-9=variations). Maximum 9 variation branches enforced at service layer.

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: parent_message_id TEXT FK to desktop_messages.id, branch_index INTEGER DEFAULT 0 (0-9). CREATE INDEX idx_messages_parent ON desktop_messages(parent_message_id). CREATE INDEX idx_messages_thread_tree ON desktop_messages(thread_id, parent_message_id, branch_index). Soft delete: deleted_at INTEGER.

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: getSiblingBranches(parentMessageId): Promise<Message[]>, getMessageTree(threadId): Promise<MessageTreeNode[]>, getPathToRoot(messageId): Promise<Message[]>. All filter out soft-deleted messages: WHERE deleted_at IS NULL.

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: async submit(threadId, parentMessageId, prompt, branchIndex?) validates branch limit (max 2 siblings), auto-assigns branchIndex, creates user message with tree metadata, assembles context (include parent + sibling branches), submits to AI service.

artifact.reason: Existing Message interface - needs parentMessageId and branchIndex fields added
Existing repository handling messages - needs tree query methods added (getSiblingBranches, getPathToRoot, etc.)
Existing thread service unit tests - reference for test patterns when adding branch logic tests

package.name: better-sqlite3
package.note: For local SQLite database with message tree

story-context.constraints: - **Desktop Implementation:** This story is Desktop-only (Electron + TypeScript). NOT backend Moku API. Implements local message tree structure in Desktop SQLite database. - **Tree Structure:** Root messages: parentMessageId = null, branchIndex = 0. Child messages: parentMessageId = parent UUID, branchIndex = 0-2. Branch siblings: same parentMessageId, different branchIndex. - **Branch Limit:** Maximum 9 variation branches per message (branchIndex 0-9). Enforce client-side before API call. Throw error "Maximum variation branches reached (max: 9)" if limit exceeded. - **Context Assembly Algorithm:** Walk up tree from current message via parentMessageId links. Collect messages in array. Reverse array for root-to-current order. Return ordered array for LLM context. Algorithm: while (current) { path.unshift(current); current = current.parentMessageId ? getMessage(current.parentMessageId) : null; } - **Soft Delete:** Messages use deleted_at timestamp (not hard delete). All tree queries filter out deleted messages: WHERE deleted_at IS NULL. - **Cache Invalidation:** Update ThreadCache to store tree structure. Invalidate branch cache when new branch created. Cache assembleContext() results for performance. - **Dependency on E1-S3:** Backend Message API with branching support (parentMessageId, branchIndex fields) must be implemented first. Desktop consumes these fields from API responses.

interface.name: Message interface
interface.signature: interface Message { id: string; threadId: string; role: 'user' | 'assistant'; content: string; timestamp: Date; parentMessageId: string | null; branchIndex: number; attachments?: Attachment[]; metadata?: MessageMetadata; status: 'pending' | 'complete' | 'error'; }

interface.name: MessageRepository.getSiblingBranches()
interface.signature: getSiblingBranches(parentMessageId: string): Promise<Message[]> - Returns all messages with same parentMessageId (different branchIndex), filtering out soft-deleted

interface.name: MessageRepository.getPathToRoot()
interface.signature: getPathToRoot(messageId: string): Promise<Message[]> - Walks up tree from message to root via parentMessageId links, returns ordered array [root, ..., current]

interface.name: assembleContext()
interface.signature: assembleContext(messageId: string): Message[] - Walks up tree, returns ordered array from root to current message for LLM context

interface.name: getNextBranchIndex()
interface.signature: getNextBranchIndex(parentMessageId: string): number - Queries existing branches, returns next available index (0-2), throws error if limit reached

tests.standards: Use vitest for unit tests. Test tree structure: create root message (parentMessageId=null), create child with parentMessageId=root.id, verify tree relationship. Test branch limit: create 3 branches (0-9) for same parent, attempt 4th, expect error. Test assembleContext: create tree (root → child1 → child2), call assembleContext(child2.id), verify returns [root, child1, child2]. Test soft delete filtering: mark message deleted_at=now, verify getSiblingBranches excludes it.

test.idea: Unit test: Create root message with parentMessageId=null, branchIndex=0, verify saved correctly
Unit test: Create child message with parentMessageId=rootId, branchIndex=0, verify forms valid tree relationship
Unit test: getSiblingBranches(parentId) - create 3 messages with same parentId, different branchIndex, verify returns all 3
Unit test: getSiblingBranches with soft-deleted sibling - create 2 siblings, mark 1 deleted (deleted_at=now), verify only returns non-deleted
Unit test: assembleContext(leafMessageId) - create tree root→child1→child2, call with child2.id, verify returns [root, child1, child2] in order
Unit test: assembleContext with branched path - create root→(branch0, branch1), verify assembleContext(branch1) returns [root, branch1] not branch0

Unit test: getNextBranchIndex(parentId) - no existing branches, verify returns 0. 1 branch exists, returns 1. 2 branches exist, returns 2. 9 branches exist, throws error 'Maximum variation branches reached'
Integration test: ThreadService.submit() with 3 existing branches - call submit with branchIndex=3, verify throws BranchLimitError before API call
Integration test: Create thread with multiple branches, query getMessagesByParentId(rootId), verify returns all immediate children
